audit_items.proof_trace: ber_strdup() at fetch.c:71 can return NULL on malloc failure; no NULL check exists between that call and ldap_pvt_hex_unescape(p) at line 80. ldap_pvt_hex_unescape immediately dereferences the parameter at url.c:1625 in the for-loop condition (*s), causing a NULL pointer dereference and crash.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker triggers memory exhaustion (e.g., via many concurrent connections) causing malloc to fail inside ber_strdup(); ldif_open_url() then passes NULL to ldap_pvt_hex_unescape(), which dereferences it at the first loop iteration, crashing the process (DoS).
